key_insight: IA-NNP learns how to stabilize difficult cohesive-zone Newton solves by correcting only active interface variables, improving convergence without changing the underlying CZM solution set.
engineering_relevance: This can make implicit fracture and delamination simulations more robust in aerospace composite structures, reducing failed increments, step cuts, and branch-convergence errors.
method: Interface-aware neural preconditioning for active cohesive interfaces
Two modes: learned initial-guess lifting and iteration-level nonlinear right preconditioning
Confidence-gated, bounded correction with root-equivalence to preserve the original CZM model
evidence_signal: Benchmarks on horizontal, circular, two-interface, and active-front cases show better difficult-increment convergence, branch recovery, and fewer failures than standard Newton and manual modification.
watch_item: The method depends on trained, interface-specific features and still relies on the original Newton solve, so robustness outside the benchmarked cohesive settings may require careful validation.
paper_title: Interface-Aware Neural Newton Preconditioning for Robust Cohesive Zone Model Simulations
